tooltip.openTrace: Open this correlation in the Trace panel

groupHint.settings: Console, identity, integrations, and runtime preferences

nav.reorderHint: Drag to reorder. Press Alt+Up or Alt+Down for keyboard reordering.
nav.hideActiveDisabled: Open another panel before hiding this one.

panelSub.agents: Live agent collaboration: status, incidents, and conversation
panelSub.provision: Watch an in-flight re-provision progress, streamed read-only
panelSub.audit: Append-only record of every terminal decision
panelSub.conversationSearch: Find authorized turns across prior sessions
panelSub.agentActivity: Per-agent timeline: which agent did what, when, and how
panelSub.handover: Who owns each of the 15 agents: accountable owners + FDAI maintainers
panelSub.blastRadius: Simulate reachable subgraph before a change
panelSub.promotionGates: Per-ActionType readiness to move from observation to enforcement (shadow → enforce)
panelSub.browserEvidence: Immutable redacted evidence from allowlisted browser captures
panelSub.dashboard: Healthy, risk, and cost, sourced from the audit log
panelSub.operatingOutcomes: Trends, baselines, and contributors for core operating metrics
panelSub.controlAssurance: Guard evidence, release blockers, and promotion readiness
panelSub.verticalOutcomes: Resilience, Change Safety, and Cost Governance results
panelSub.trustRouting: Tier mix, target bands, and routed event evidence
panelSub.rules: Every policy in the rule catalog, faceted
panelSub.workflowBuilder: Browse built-in workflows or design a new one by chatting
panelSub.llmCost: Measured calls and tokens by model and workload
panelSub.capabilities: Declared capability contracts, side-effect classes, roles, and default modes
panelSub.skills: Installed runtime skill metadata, dependencies, eligibility, and load diagnostics
panelSub.onboarding: Provisioned resources and required role assignments
panelSub.processes: Workflow runtime state and dynamic ontology-backed views
panelSub.workflowApps: Published workflow-specific read surfaces and run history
panelSub.schedulerRuns: Task dispatch history from the configured scheduler ledger
panelSub.automationBlueprints: Evidence-backed recurring-work suggestions awaiting operator review
panelSub.scheduledContinuations: Scoped conversation anchors for exact scheduled results and evidence
panelSub.conversationDelivery: Reply latency, retries, duplicate risk, abandonment, and adapter health
panelSub.operatorMemory: Scope-bounded approved guidance, provenance, expiry, and supersession
panelSub.reports: Declarative live boards rendered from the report catalog
panelSub.documents: Upload, scan, protect, and index governed documents
panelSub.settings: Display, accessibility, and local console preferences
panelSub.settingsModels: T1 and T2 models, provisioning, latency, and personal routing
panelSub.settingsIam: Human roles, capabilities, and governed access requests
panelSub.incidents: Incident status, disposition, and recovery history
panelSub.rca: Root cause: evidence-backed hypothesis, citations, and response plan
panelSub.scope: Effective monitoring and automated-action scope by subscription and RG

waterfall.emptyBody: No correlated activity for this agent yet. Clear the filter to see the full waterfall.
waterfall.label: Agent activity waterfall
waterfall.selectBody: Pick any agent step on the left to see its full lifecycle - when the event was sent and received, how long it queued and worked, what it consumed and produced, and the recorded decision.

live.title: Observed live activity
live.waiting: {subject} is connected and waiting for an ingress event. Runtime heartbeats below prove connectivity but are not operational work.
live.noFrames: No runtime frame has arrived during this browser session.
live.retention: Showing the newest {count} of {total} in-memory frames.

main.runtimeStillConnected: Live runtime activity remains connected below.
main.sampleBody: {count} visible row(s) are synthetic UI evidence. They remain available in Audit and Trace, but do not represent live observation and do not create Incident roster entries.
main.unknownAgent: Agent {agent} is not in the fixed pantheon or the loaded audit activity.
main.latestRows: Showing the latest {count} audit rows; older activity is available in the Audit log.
main.noAudit: No durable agent audit activity yet
main.noAuditBody: Live runtime state remains available for a selected agent. Audit events appear here only after the control loop records them.
main.noMatchesBody: Widen the window or clear the layer, verb, agent, and search filters.
main.noSelectionEvidence: No live runtime node or attributed audit row is available for this selection.
main.noRuntimeFrame: No runtime state frame has been observed for {agent}.
main.noActiveCorrelation: There is no active correlation or incident.
main.noCorrelationAudit: Correlation {correlation} has no attributed audit row in the current window.
main.noDurableAudit: No durable audit row has been attributed to this agent in the current window.

pantheon.subtitle: The fixed 15-agent organization, enriched with each agent's current runtime state.
pantheon.sourceLead: Cards come from the fork-locked pantheon registry. Runtime state comes from
pantheon.directoryHint: Compare ownership, dependencies, and runtime work by layer.
pantheon.organizationHint: Follow reporting lines, inspect live state, and open an agent for detail.

layer.pipeline: Sense, judge, approve, execute, and recover

pantheon.reportingTreeHint: Every reporting line is projected from the registry.

handover.subtitle: Who owns each of the 15 agents now that FDAI runs the control plane - accountable owners, FDAI maintainers, and ownership handover coverage. Read-only.
handover.unavailable: The stewardship endpoint is not wired on this deployment. Set ReadApiConfig.stewardship_map in the composition root to enable it.
handover.noMaintainer: No maintainer configured - at least 1 is required.
handover.oneMaintainer: Only 1 maintainer - 2 are recommended for succession safety.
handover.proposeLead: editing the handover map is a governance draft PR (Owner-gated). Edit
handover.proposeTail: and open a PR; the console never writes it directly. On merge, the affected agents' owners and the maintainer are notified and the change is recorded in the audit log.

header.title: Agent fleet
header.descriptionLead: Live work across the fixed 15-agent pantheon. Inspect current tasks, open each agent's evidence timeline, or ask about its grounded context. State combines the durable

task.collecting: Ingesting and correlating signals for an event
task.deciding: Issuing a verdict at the risk gate

odin.title: Master Planner
odin.summary: Cross-vertical arbitration; final tie-breaker before a verdict is finalized.

thor.title: Responder
thor.summary: Dispatches verdicts and is the sole privileged executor; never judges.

forseti.title: Judge
forseti.summary: Issues the verdict (auto / hil / deny) after cross-check, verifier, and grounding.

bragi.title: Narrator
bragi.summary: Conversational-port translator only; never calls an executor directly.

var.title: Approver
var.summary: Human-in-the-loop approval principal; stays distinct from Thor.

huginn.title: Event Collector
huginn.summary: Owns real-time resource discovery ingress and normalizes events; cloud I/O stays in adapters.

heimdall.title: Observer
heimdall.summary: Monitors discovery freshness and coverage, then correlates signals into findings.

njord.title: Cost
njord.summary: Cost / FinOps domain specialist; advises Forseti and does not execute.

freyr.title: Capacity
freyr.summary: Capacity / forecast domain specialist; advises Forseti and does not execute.

loki.title: Chaos
loki.summary: Chaos / resilience specialist; schedules experiments and advises Forseti.

mimir.title: Rule Steward
mimir.summary: Owns the rule catalog; grounds Forseti's judgments in current rules.

muninn.title: Memory
muninn.summary: Memory / context store; supplies prior-incident context to Forseti and Bragi.

saga.title: Auditor
saga.summary: Append-only audit of every terminal state; materializes handoffs.

norns.title: Learner
norns.summary: Learns from audited outcomes and proposes rule revisions to Mimir.

roster.interpretationBody: Engaged counts agents handling a pipeline stage now, not every subscribed runtime loop. Idle agents wake on their topics. This console observes work; it does not approve or execute actions. Live delta source: {source}. Incident history comes from the durable audit projection.
roster.discoveryBody: Azure resource writes and deletes enter through Event Hubs and Huginn continuously. The Inventory sync job still runs Azure Resource Graph with ARM fallback every 6 hours to reconcile missed signals. Heimdall monitors freshness and coverage. The local harness uses read-only Azure CLI discovery by default.

workflow.selectIncident: Select an incident to watch the agents collaborate.
workflow.ask: Ask the deck about this incident
workflow.askHint: Questions are read-only; a command opens a proposal (judged, never executed here).

context.purpose: The live 15-agent pantheon. Each incident is one collaboration. This read-only view explains selected work and opens governed proposals without executing them here.
context.headline: {incidents} incident(s) - {agents} agent(s) engaged

error.unknownAgent: Agent {agent} is not in the fixed pantheon.
error.unknownIncident: Incident {incident} is not present in the retained agent stream.

browserEvidence.subtitle: Read-only provenance for bounded browser captures. Extracted content remains untrusted.
browserEvidence.readOnlyBody: Capture, promotion, approval, and execution controls are not available in this view.

workflowApps.subtitle: Published, read-only workflow surfaces. Apps reuse governed Process journals and ViewSpecs; they never add action buttons or remote code.
workflowApps.emptyBody: Draft and shadow manifests stay out of Operations until their separate publication review completes.
workflowApps.notFound: The requested workflow app is not published or is not visible to this principal.
workflowApps.noRunsBody: The app is available, but this workflow has no Process snapshots in the current evidence store.
workflowApps.viewPurpose: A read-only catalog of published workflow apps and their governed Process evidence.

reports.subtitle: Catalog-driven read-only boards. Select a report, set its declared variables, and render the current evidence window.
reports.empty: No reports are registered on this deployment.
reports.notFound: The selected report is not available.
reports.readyHint: Complete the required variables, then render the report.
reports.datasourceUnavailable: The report rendered, but its datasource is not wired. No fresh evidence is claimed.
reports.registry: Console renderers cover {supported} of {total} registered widget types. Unsupported types remain explicitly unavailable.
reports.viewPurpose: Read-only catalog reports rendered by the reporting subsystem. Widgets display server-owned evidence and never execute actions.

provision.subtitle: Read-only progress from the active provisioning stream
provision.viewPurpose: Observe provisioning progress and terminal outcomes from the read-only stream. This screen cannot start, retry, or alter provisioning.
provision.readOnlySuffix: This screen renders progress and never executes provisioning.
provision.notObserved: No provisioning run has been observed on this stream. A connected stream alone does not imply 0% progress.
provision.idlePrefix: No provisioning is in progress. This screen wakes when the stream carries a

onboardingView.viewPurpose: Check whether required runtime resources and executor role assignments are present before FDAI can operate.
onboardingView.notConfigured: The Azure onboarding probe is not configured. These gaps describe the required baseline, not observed tenant state.
onboardingView.probeFailed: The configured Azure onboarding probe failed. No tenant readiness observation is available.

conversationSearch.title: Conversation search
conversationSearch.subtitle: Authorized prior turns with source and lineage
conversationSearch.recordedTimeHelp: Enter local time. Search converts the range to UTC.

processesView.subtitle: Workflow run status from authoritative Process snapshots and append-only journals. Execution remains outside the console.
processesView.viewPurpose: Read-only workflow Process snapshots and ontology-backed dynamic views.
processesView.emptyBody: Process runs appear here after the workflow runtime records them.
processesView.selectJournal: Select a process to inspect its runtime journal.
processesView.noViewSpec: No workflow-specific ViewSpec is registered. The runtime snapshot and journal above remain available for every workflow.

rca.subtitle: Root-cause analysis for one incident, projected read-only from the append-only audit log. An RCA result is an evidence-backed hypothesis, never an authoritative final decision; execution eligibility stays with the safety check (risk gate) and verifier.
rca.idle: Enter a correlation id and fetch to see its root-cause analysis.
rca.empty: No RCA hypothesis has been recorded for this incident yet.
rca.abstainedNotice: This hypothesis is not supported by evidence. It is held for review and routed to human approval, and is never treated as a confident cause.
rca.noResponse: No linked response action has been recorded for this incident yet.
rca.viewPurpose: Per-incident root-cause analysis projected from the audit log: tiered evidence-backed hypotheses, citations, causal reasoning, and the linked response plan. Read-only; an RCA hypothesis answers why, never execute.

scope.subtitle: Effective monitoring and automated-action scope by subscription and resource group. Read-only: authoring a change generates a policy-as-code artifact you submit as a fix / config PR (GitOps); the console never writes scope.
scope.notServed: The effective-scope view is not served by this deployment.
scope.viewPurpose: Effective monitoring scope (what FDAI observes) and automated-action scope (what an autonomous action may touch) by subscription and resource group, plus the hard RG-scoped executor identity boundary. Read-only; scope changes ship as policy-as-code PRs.

axisHint.monitoring: Which subscriptions and resource groups FDAI observes.
axisHint.action: Which subscriptions and resource groups an autonomous (non-approval) action may touch.

scope.emptyAxis: No scope entries for this axis.
scope.executorHint: The executor is an RG-scoped, action-whitelisted managed identity. This hard IAM ceiling cannot be widened by any governance scope.
scope.builderHint: Compose an include/exclude selection, then submit the generated artifact as a fix / config PR. Nothing is applied from the console.
scope.builderEmpty: Add at least one entry to generate a scope artifact.
scope.artifactHint: Open a PR with this artifact. Approval, audit, and rollback come from Git; the console issues no privileged write.
scope.copyFailedHint: Clipboard access failed. Select the visible artifact and copy it manually.

incidents.subtitle: Incident-centric status and recovery history from the append-only audit log. Read-only; actions and approvals stay in Git and ChatOps.
incidents.select: Select an incident to inspect its recovery history.
incidents.selectNamed: Select {title} and show its recovery history
incidents.emptyHistory: No audit entries for this incident.
incidents.viewPurpose: Incident-centric roster and recovery history projected from the append-only audit log. Read-only.
incidents.historyShown: Showing {shown} of {total} history entries. Open the filtered audit log to continue browsing.

documents.subtitle: Upload governed documents through quarantine, protection checks, extraction, and indexing.
documents.visibilityNotice: This upload is not private to you. People who can access {collection} in FDAI may be able to view its filename, preview, extracted text, and citations. Do not upload secrets or content that this audience may not access.
documents.visibilityConfirm: I understand who can access this collection and confirm that these files are appropriate for that audience.
documents.dropHint: Files remain unavailable until mandatory safety checks complete.
documents.limits: Formats: {formats} · Maximum: {size} per file · {count} files per batch
documents.fileTooLarge: This file exceeds the advertised size limit.
documents.processingTimeout: Document processing did not finish within the polling window.
documents.handoverDraftSummary: {mappings} mappings, {unresolved} unresolved people, {unmapped} unmapped agents. This YAML is a draft and is never applied automatically.

labs.subtitle: Development-only design and UI experiments. These tools never mutate managed resources.
labs.logoLabDescription: Inspect the selected Cloud Aperture mark and alternative brand lockups.

operatorMemory.subtitle: Read-only governance view of durable, scope-bounded operator guidance. Changes require the approved human-approval or ChatOps workflow.
operatorMemory.unavailable: No durable operator-memory provider is registered for this Azure environment.
operatorMemory.empty: No operator-memory entries match these filters.
operatorMemory.noCompactions: No memory compaction candidates are recorded.

settings.subtitle: Display and accessibility preferences stored in this browser.
settings.themeHint: Applied immediately across every console view.
settings.languageHint: Reloads the console so every navigation label uses the selected language.
settings.motionHint: Reduce decorative animation while preserving live state updates.
settings.showTokenUsageHint: Show the tokens spent per reply on the chat answer badge, next to model and latency.
settings.readApiHint: The read-only endpoint supplying this console session.
settings.readApiLivenessHint: Process response only. This does not prove that operational data paths are healthy.
settings.readPathHint: Authenticated read-model access used by the console overview.
settings.dataSourcesHint: Composition-owned availability and provenance. Route presence alone never proves evidence health.
settings.contextTimezoneInvalid: Enter a valid IANA timezone, such as UTC or Asia/Seoul.
settings.briefingHourInvalid: Daily briefing hour must be a whole number from 0 through 23.
settings.contextPartialSave: User preferences were saved, but response defaults failed: {error}
settings.contextDescription: Account-scoped narrator preferences and explicit learner consent. Display preferences remain browser-local.
settings.answerDetailHint: Default response density for Bragi. A current-turn instruction still wins.
settings.answerFormatHint: Default response structure when the current turn does not request one.
settings.answerPreferencesHint: Disable stored defaults without changing the language or deleting the profile.
settings.timezoneHint: IANA timezone used by recurring briefings.
settings.learnerAccessHint: Allow Norns to use turn bodies for pattern extraction. Off by default.
settings.openingBriefingHint: Show high and critical issues from the previous 24 hours once per conversation.
settings.startConversationHint: Start one Command Deck conversation before enabling this confirmed policy.
settings.memoryDescription: Only explicitly confirmed facts are stored. Removing one also removes its ontology projection.

models.subtitle: Resolved T1 and T2 models, lifecycle health, latency evidence, narrator preference, and governed T2 policy drafts.
models.automationHint: Discovery and provisioning are resolved at bootstrap. This page observes status and never provisions resources.
models.narratorPreferenceHint: Choose Auto for latency routing or pin one available T1 narrator for your account.
models.routingHint: Latest selected deployment, redacted failover reason, cooldown, and recovery from durable routing telemetry.
models.noRoutingState: No durable model routing state has been recorded.
models.noHealthTransitions: No health transitions are recorded for this role.
models.endpointInventoryHint: Verified direct, API Management, PTU, and self-hosted routes. Endpoint and credential references remain hidden.
models.noEndpoints: No verified model endpoint bindings are available.
models.webSearchHint: Deployment-wide conversational evidence through the configured Azure Responses provider.
models.webSearchEnabledHint: Allow evidence-backed public-web lookup for eligible conversations.
models.allowedDomainsHint: Enter one host per line, without scheme, path, port, or wildcard. Maximum 100.
models.webSearchBoundaryWarning: Evidence lookup with Bing may send queries and retrieved data outside your compliance or geographic boundary. Keep the allowlist scoped to approved public sources.
models.domainRequired: At least one allowed domain is required while web search is enabled.
models.domainLimit: Allowed domains cannot exceed 100 unique hosts.
models.domainInvalid: Enter hosts only. Remove schemes, paths, ports, and wildcards from: {domains}
models.webSearchConflict: Web search settings changed elsewhere. The latest server values were reloaded; review them before saving again.
models.webSearchConflictReloadFailed: Web search settings changed elsewhere, but the latest values could not be reloaded.
models.capabilitiesHint: Resolved assignments remain system governed. Use the T2 policy draft above to propose a reviewed catalog change.
models.noCapabilities: No resolved model capabilities are available.

settings.integrationsSubtitle: Read-only status for identity, delivery, and operator-channel integrations.
settings.integrationsPurpose: Inspect identity and delivery integration status without changing runtime configuration.
settings.diagnosticsSubtitle: Runtime and authentication details for this console session.
settings.diagnosticsPurpose: Verify console liveness and authenticated read-path availability.
settings.diagnosticsHeadline: Read API: {health}; KPI read path: {readPath}; authentication: {mode}.
settings.invalidLivenessResponse: The read API returned an invalid liveness response.
settings.entraHint: OIDC sign-in and API App Roles for the current tenant.
settings.githubAppHint: Creates governed draft pull requests without exposing a personal access token to the browser.
settings.teamsHint: Carries human approval requests through the configured ChatOps channel.
settings.integrationProbeUnavailable: No delivery status adapter is registered on this deployment. This page does not infer health from configuration alone.
settings.authenticationHint: The identity source used for this console session.
settings.principalHint: Display identity only. Authorization uses the stable Entra object id and App Roles.

iam.subtitle: Human identity roles and effective capabilities. Access changes remain approval-gated and never call Microsoft Graph from the browser.
iam.invalidTab: This identity and access view is not registered. Choose one of the available tabs above.
iam.currentAccessHint: Verified identity and effective FDAI authorization for this session.
iam.localCeilingHint: The local API applies a fixed development role ceiling. It does not replace or rename your signed-in Entra identity.
iam.directoryRosterHint: FDAI role groups and their current directory members.
iam.noRosterEntries: No people or groups are available.
iam.rosterUnavailable: The identity-provider roster is unavailable: {error}. Showing principals referenced by access requests instead.
iam.ownerTabsHint: Users and access requests are available to Owners.
iam.roleRequestHint: This creates a governed access request. It does not change the role immediately.
iam.roleRequestJustification: Explain the operational need (at least 20 characters)
iam.usersOwnerOnly: Only an Owner can view the directory roster or change role assignments.
iam.loadFailed: Failed to load identity and access data: {error}
iam.rolesHint: Fixed FDAI App Roles and the capabilities each role grants.
iam.directoryOwnerOnly: Owner access is required to search the configured identity provider.
iam.searchPrompt: Search the configured identity provider to add a user.
iam.usersHint: This projection shows the verified signed-in principal and users referenced by visible access requests. Entra remains the membership authority.
iam.noUsers: No users are visible in this projection.
iam.roleTableCaption: FDAI human App Roles and their cumulative capabilities
iam.assignmentBoundaryHint: Users receive FDAI roles through allowlisted Entra security groups. The console creates governed access requests; an Owner applies approved membership changes outside the browser.
iam.requestsHint: Submitting records an approval-gated request and audit entry. It does not change Entra membership.
iam.requestPermissionRequired: Contributor or higher is required to submit an access request.
iam.reviewJustificationMinimum: Enter at least 20 characters before reviewing this request ({count}/20).
iam.requestsRestrictedHint: Only an Owner can view who requested access or review decisions.
iam.ownerPermissionRequired: You do not have permission to view access request identities. Owner role is required.
iam.requestsAdminHint: People waiting for FDAI access and their requested roles.

roleCapabilities.reader: View the operator console and read audit evidence.
roleCapabilities.contributor: Reader capabilities plus author governed draft pull requests.
roleCapabilities.approver: Contributor capabilities plus governance review and runtime approval.
roleCapabilities.owner: Approver capabilities plus group membership, kill-switch, and infrastructure administration.
roleCapabilities.breakGlass: Isolated emergency access and kill-switch capabilities; never a normal elevation path.

accessRequired.title: Request console access
accessRequired.description: Your identity is verified, but no FDAI role is assigned. Request Reader access and an administrator can review it in Settings > Identity and access.
accessRequired.messagePlaceholder: Add context for the administrator reviewing this request.

statusDescription.pending: An administrator can review this request in Settings > Identity and access.
statusDescription.approved: Provider assignment is still required. Access remains blocked until it is completed and you sign in again.
statusDescription.rejected: Review the decision and submit a new request with additional context when appropriate.

accessRequired.checkFailed: FDAI could not verify your access. No console data was opened.
accessRequired.recoveryHint: Retry the read API connection. If the identity session expired, sign in again.

login.subtitle: Autonomous cloud operations control plane
login.localSubtitle: Choose an identity session or continue with the local development ceiling.
login.devBypassFailed: Local dev access is unavailable for this API ({error}). Sign in with Microsoft Entra ID.
login.localFoot: The dev bypass is session-scoped and never available in production.
login.foot: Read-only operator console. Changes are governed and high-risk actions require human approval.

deck.label: Command deck
deck.noConversations: Start a conversation to keep it in this tab's history.
deck.removeCachedConversationHint: Remove this tab's cached transcript. Audit history is unchanged.
deck.inputPlaceholder: Ask anything, or type / for commands
deck.intro: Ask about anything currently visible - tiles, KPIs, approvals, audit rows, promotion status, impact scope, or ontology. I ground every answer in the snapshot on the right.

digest.label: What the deck sees
digest.title: What I see
digest.empty: No route has published a view snapshot. Open Live, Dashboard, Audit, Approvals, Trace, Impact scope, Promotion gates, or Ontology.
digest.records: + {count} record(s) available for the answerer to search{breakdown}.

fact.eps: Events per second the control loop is processing (60s rolling).
fact.sessionTotal: Total events seen since this live session started.
fact.sessionDuration: How long this live session has been running.
fact.tilesActive: Tiles currently showing an in-flight action.
fact.tilesEmpty: Unused tiles in the cockpit grid.
fact.tilesShadow: Tiles running in shadow mode (judge-and-log, no execution).
fact.tierT0: Share routed to T0 - deterministic policy (target 70-80%).
fact.tierT1: Share routed to T1 - lightweight similarity / small model (15-20%).
fact.tierT2: Share routed to T2 - frontier-model reasoning, novel cases only (5-10%).
fact.gateAuto: Actions the risk gate auto-executed (low risk).
fact.gateHil: High-risk actions routed to human approval.
fact.gateAbstain: Cases the gate abstained on - no autonomous action taken.
fact.attentionHil: Items waiting on a human approval.
fact.attentionStuck: Actions stuck without progress past their budget.
fact.verticalsChange: Change Safety events (safe change, drift remediation).
fact.verticalsUnknown: Events not yet classified into a vertical.

tooltip.backToGeneral: Back to the general screen deck
tooltip.backendFallback: Model unavailable ({mode}); using the deterministic answerer

workflowBuilder.subtitle: Browse and design catalog workflows, or author a governed Python VM task. YAML changes remain PR-native; Python source must validate and stage as an immutable artifact before a shadow plan, schedule, or typed run request.

overview.subtitle: Operating posture, measured outcomes, control assurance, and required attention.

section.outcomesHint: Results measured against the pinned baseline on the same evidence window.
section.assuranceHint: Guard evidence that permits, blocks, or holds autonomous operation.
section.organizationHint: Accountable separation of sensing, judgment, approval, execution, and audit.
section.verticalsHint: Resilience, Change Safety, and Cost Governance results over the same window.

status.label: Executive operating posture
status.summary: {rate}% autonomous resolution - Approvals pending: {hil} - {escapes} policy escapes
status.simulatedSummary: Simulation: {rate}% autonomous resolution - Approvals pending: {hil} - {escapes} policy escapes
status.fallback: {events} audit events - Approvals pending: {hil} - autonomy evidence unavailable

evidence.unavailableHint: This section remains visible, but no measured value is inferred until its source is connected.

hero.title: Autonomous cloud operations
hero.window: Proven over the last {days} days - {samples} events
hero.syntheticHint: Dev harness: synthetic measurement, not a measured claim

attention.label: Requires attention
attention.title: {count} signals require review
attention.hilHint: Approval remains outside the read-only console.
attention.escapeHint: Review the promotion evidence before enforcement.
attention.unknownHint: The console does not infer a healthy state from missing guard data.
attention.clearHint: No approval backlog, policy escape, or failed guard is recorded.

organization.label: Agent organization
organization.title: Autonomy with separation of duties
organization.summary: The agent organization resolved {rate}% autonomously and held {hil} for human approval.
organization.simulatedSummary: The simulation models {rate}% autonomous resolution and {hil} items held for human approval.

trend.summary: Trend from {start} to {end}, a change of {delta}.

tier.label: Trust tiers
tier.groupLabel: Trust tier mix compared with target bands

approvals.subtitle: High-risk actions waiting for a human approver. Approvals happen in ChatOps; this console remains read-only.
approvals.emptyBody: All autonomous decisions are within the safety check's automatic envelope right now.
approvals.showingLatest: Showing the latest {shown} of {total} pending approvals.
approvals.mechanicsBody: High-risk work stays parked until a distinct approver acts in ChatOps. Rejects and timeouts are audited; this screen only renders evidence.
approvals.filterPlaceholder: Filter by action, resource, rule, or event
approvals.noMatchBody: Clear the filter to return to the full pending queue.
approvals.detailsRoleTitle: Approval details require the Approver role
approvals.detailsRoleBody: This Reader view exposes only the authoritative queue count. Review the target, proposed action, and requester in the signed ChatOps approval card.
